---
title: "X corruption when screen is turned off"
date: 2008-06-20
forum: General Help
---

### Post by cyran on 2008-06-20
I have Hardy on a Thinkpad T60 with a Radeon X1400 graphics card, running the repository's fglrx proprietary driver. I rather foolishly attempted to run the Spore Creature Creator, which requires the latest DirectX 9, under Wine, and X became unusable. I booted into recovery-mode, repaired X automatically, and it works fine now, except for this problem.

When I close my laptop lid, the screen is turned off automatically, even if I set the power-manager preference for "when laptop lid is closed" to "do nothing." When I open it, for less than half a second it looks fine--it shows the log-back-in-to-unlock-this-computer screen--but it immediately changes into randomly colored rectangles on a flashing screen.

Presing Ctrl+Alt+Backspace, or Ctrl+Alt+F6 and then Ctrl+Alt+F7, can fix it temporarily. Can anyone help?

P.S.: I installed [etckeeper]("http://packages.ubuntu.com/hardy/etckeeper") a while ago, so I know that in revision 56, when wine, somehow, messed with my X configuration and I recovered it, at the same time it modified the file '/etc/ati/amdpcsdb'. I reverted the change manually, and re-repaired X, but the problem still persists.

The change was:

```
=== modified file 'ati/amdpcsdb'
--- ati/amdpcsdb	2008-04-27 21:47:06 +0000
+++ ati/amdpcsdb	2008-06-18 16:36:33 +0000
@@ -18,12 +18,17 @@
 R6LCD_RETURNALLBIOSMODES=V1
 TVEnableOverscan=V1
 DALPowerPlayOptions=V1
-DALLastSelected=V2
+DALLastSelected=V1
 DALLastConnected=V2
 DALLastTypes=V11
 
 
 
 
-DALCurrentObjectData=R010200000000
+DALCurrentObjectData=R010100000000
 DALR6 LCD_MaxModeInfo=R00000000780500001A040000000000003C000000
+DAL_CRTColorTemperatureSource00000000=R0000000064190000
+[AMDPCSROOT/SYSTEM/DDX/RECENTMODE/SCREEN00]
+Width=V1024
+Height=V768
+Refresh=V60


```

---
